response.justification: Strong, versatile senior engineer with real distributed-systems exposure (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of requests/second) and solid software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development). However, the role's core hard requirement is 8 years of C++, and the candidate's C++ work is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments plus a university thesis. The primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not C++, and there is no cloud compute platform or formal technical-leadership experience. The C++ and large-scale cloud-platform gaps are decisive against the minimum qualifications.
strengths: Real distributed/low-latency systems experience (millions of req/sec micro-services)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, DevOps tooling, mentoring/teaching
Broad senior full-stack background with 13+ years across many domains
concerns: Only ~6 months of hands-on C++; far short of the 8-year minimum
No cloud compute platform or production cloud-architecture experience
No formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers
